items.title: Who Are the Long-Term Winners of AI?
items.content: This article discusses the long-term winners of AI, moving beyond the initial productivity gains to focus on companies that can truly capture lasting value. It argues that market power, the ability to capitalize on unmet demand, and careful selection of sectors will be crucial for sustained profitability from AI. The article highlights that while AI lowers costs, competitive dynamics can erode profits unless companies have strong market positions or can unlock new markets through reduced prices.

items.title: Robeco Institutional Asset Management B.V. Grows Position in PTC Inc. $PTC
items.content: Robeco Institutional Asset Management B.V. significantly increased its stake in PTC Inc. by 119.5% in Q3, now owning 361,938 shares valued at $73.48 million. Institutional investors collectively hold 95.14% of PTC, while insiders have recently sold shares totaling over $10 million. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for PTC with a consensus price target of $205.92.

items.title: Enterprise infrastructure shift: Dell and Microsoft drive AI
items.content: Dell Technologies Inc. and Microsoft Corp. are collaborating to reshape enterprise infrastructure, making AI a central component. Their initiatives, including Dell's AI Factory and Microsoft's Azure integrations, aim to simplify AI adoption, move beyond proof-of-concepts to scalable deployment, and build infrastructure optimized for agentic AI. This partnership signals a major shift towards "infrastructure as an AI platform" in the enterprise IT market.

items.title: The Smartest Dividend ETF to Buy With $2,000 Right Now
items.content: This article recommends the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) as a smart dividend investment. The JEPQ ETF utilizes a covered call strategy on top Nasdaq-100 technology stocks, offering a current dividend yield of over 10% monthly. It provides a way for investors to get significant dividend income from high-performing tech companies, although it sacrifices potential for massive stock price appreciation.

items.title: McGraw Hill Appoints Philip Moyer as CEO
items.content: McGraw Hill has announced the appointment of Philip Moyer as its new President and CEO, effective February 9, 2026. Moyer, who brings extensive experience in technology and AI, will succeed Simon Allen, who will transition to Chair of the Board of Directors. This leadership change is expected to leverage Moyer's background in AI-driven transformation to further McGraw Hill's growth and innovation in educational solutions.

items.content: Alphabet shares (GOOGL.US) supported Wall Street sentiment, rising 3% due to advancements in autonomous vehicles and AI, despite China's advice against Nvidia chip purchases. Meanwhile, oil prices fell below $60 per barrel on weaker demand risks, and precious metals like gold, silver, and platinum experienced significant downturns. Bitcoin also lost momentum, failing to breach $95,000 amidst a bearish market outlook.

This transformation integrates all order channels onto a single cloud-based data backbone, improving efficiency, cutting training time, and enabling operations through outages. The company anticipates higher check averages, advanced AI-driven analytics, and increased digital sales as part of its "Jack on Track" initiative.

items.content: Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) and Hexagon have announced a collaboration to accelerate the development of humanoid robots for manufacturing and inspection, leveraging AI and cloud tools. This partnership aims to address labor shortages and enhance data-driven production and AI-based automation across various industries, starting with automotive and aerospace. Microsoft shares rose on Wednesday following the announcement.

The market is being redefined by AI and Machine Learning, with trends like multi-orbit compatibility and software-defined modems driving growth, while high initial costs and regulatory hurdles present restraints. Key players include Gilat Satellite Networks Ltd., Novelsat, and Comtech EF Data Corporation.

items.title: Microsoft is company behind $1B data center proposed in Lowell Township
items.content: Microsoft has identified itself as the company proposing a $1 billion data center in Lowell Township, Michigan, after earlier confidentiality agreements. The company plans to engage with the community regarding the project, which is still in its early stages with details like design and timeline yet to be determined. The project would be located at Covenant Business Park if it moves forward.

items.title: Silver price slumps hit SLV ETF and silver miner stocks as traders brace for US jobs data
items.content: Silver prices and related stocks, including the iShares Silver Trust (SLV) and major silver miners, experienced a significant drop. This downturn is attributed to profit-taking after a recent surge and anticipation of Friday's U.S. payrolls report, which could impact interest rate expectations and the dollar. Traders are also wary of upcoming commodity index rebalancing flows that could force silver futures selling.

items.title: Microsoft Dips 9% in 3 Months: 3 Reasons Why the Stock is Still a Buy
items.content: Microsoft shares have dropped 9% in the last three months, presenting a buying opportunity for long-term investors. This decline occurred despite strong Q1 fiscal 2026 results, primarily due to concerns about high capital expenditure for AI and questions regarding its monetization. However, the company's robust positions in cloud computing, AI, and enterprise software, along with its strong financial health, suggest significant future upside.

items.title: Solana ETF Sees $1.9 Million Walk Out the Door as Investors Reassess Crypto Risk
items.content: The Solana ETF (SOLZ) experienced a significant outflow of $1.9 million on January 2, 2026, representing nearly 1.5% of its assets under management. This outflow suggests increased investor caution following a volatile period for Solana (SOL-USD), which has seen a 36.38% drop in value over the past three months. The fund's current AUM stands at $128.96 million, with future flows into SOLZ expected to indicate investor confidence in Solana's long-term prospects.

items.title: Riot Platforms offloads $161M in Bitcoin in December amid strategy shift
items.content: Riot Platforms sold 1,818 Bitcoin for $161.6 million in December as part of a strategic shift away from solely Bitcoin mining. The company aims to monetize its power and data center infrastructure by supporting artificial intelligence workloads, and will transition to quarterly disclosures. This move reflects a broader trend among Bitcoin miners to seek additional revenue streams beyond BTC production, especially after the 2024 halving.

items.title: New browser tool hides ChatGPT data as AI veteran lines up to lead Vocodia
items.content: Vocodia Holdings Corp (OTC: VHAI) announced its intent to acquire a 51% stake in Privacy Pal LLC, a developer of browser-based AI data protection. This strategic move aims to integrate PrivacyPal's privacy technology with Vocodia's conversational AI. Upon the closing of the transaction, tech industry veteran Jason Melo will become Vocodia's CEO, while current CEO Brian Podolak will transition to Chief Visionary Officer, focusing on product innovation.
